original_rubric_note: Produced before monorepo migration, when the rubric lived at E:/AI/cognitive-load-audit/cognitive-load-audit.md.

surfaces_audited: + menu (files / connectors / research / web search hub)

context.task: audit, not a real user task
context.dense_state: Recents list with 16+ visible chats; one chat opened with rendered interactive widget (compound interest calculator) representing a typical 'AI-generated artifact embedded in conversation' state
context.low_bandwidth_state: auditor not in a self-reported constrained state — see OQ-04
ai_features_present: chat with adaptive model selection (Opus 4.7 / Sonnet 4.6)
context.method: Path 1 live navigation via Claude-in-Chrome MCP; ~1 hour active inspection

text_load.notes: Compose placeholder, settings labels, + menu label all clear and action-oriented. 'Dyslexic friendly' previewed in face — small UX win.

measurable_defaults.notes: No first-hand CSS/contrast measurement collected. Visual inspection of body text suggests acceptable, but unverified against WCAG 2.2 1.4.12 thresholds.

hidden_load.notes: One High; below the 3-High threshold. Sidebar collapse displaces visual-recognition load to recall, but a11y tree preserves labels for screen reader users.

state_shift.notes: Critical: no in-chat one-action low-bandwidth control. Partial offset: sidebar-collapse state persists across reload (P-01). One Critical triggers section Fail.

ai_compression_risk.notes: One Critical (CL-04, web search active without provenance indicator) + one High (CL-03, confident numeric output without source/assumption trace — classified as provenance ambiguity per Section 4 severity precondition since no source was claimed) + one Low (auto-titled chats). One Critical triggers Fail. Summary-of-source surface untested — see OQ-02.

power_preservation.notes: + menu compresses 8 features into one hub; collapsed sidebar reflows chat content. Section 5's 'simple mode removes power' pattern does NOT manifest on claude.ai.

configuration_cost.notes: One High (4-click + page-nav path to dyslexia-friendly font) and two Mediums (full-page settings nav loses chat context; no Accessibility section). Below threshold for Fail but the friction stack is real.

evidence.notes: All findings observed in live navigation, but auditor was not in a self-reported constrained state. Should be addressed with a human auditor pass before this audit's findings ship as user-facing recommendations.

findings.title: Sidebar collapse strips visual labels and removes chat history from view
findings.evidence_summary: Confirmed via baseline + collapsed screenshots and a11y tree comparison.

findings.title: No in-chat one-action low-bandwidth control
findings.evidence_summary: Full a11y tree of chat surface inspected; no relevant controls beyond compose, +, model, voice.

findings.title: Confident numeric output with no source-recovery or assumption-trace affordance
findings.severity_note: Provenance ambiguity per Section 4 severity precondition (no source was promised, claimed, or fetched — generated content). High, not Critical.
findings.evidence_summary: Widget rendered with $20,194 / 4.0x figures and silent default inputs; message action bar has no 'show source' or 'show assumptions' affordance.

findings.title: Web search runs by default with no provenance indicator in response
findings.evidence_summary: + menu shows 'Web search' checked by default; inspected response shows no citations or domain markers.

findings.title: Reaching dyslexia-friendly font from a chat takes 4 clicks + scroll + full-page nav
findings.surface: chat -> profile menu -> Settings -> /settings/general -> font dropdown
findings.evidence_summary: Counted live; URL changes from /chat/... to /settings/general; Chat font option visible with 'Dyslexic friendly' in dropdown.

findings.title: Settings opens via full-page navigation, breaking chat task context
findings.evidence_summary: URL change observed; chat surface fully hidden behind /settings/general.

findings.title: No dedicated Accessibility section in Settings; reading controls bundled into General > Preferences
findings.evidence_summary: Settings left-nav has 9 sections; none named Accessibility/Display/Reading. Chat font lives below Profile in General page.

findings.title: Auto-generated chat title silently rewrites the user's original question
findings.evidence_summary: User input 'Show me how compound interest works' compared against chat title 'How compound interest works' (Claude-generated). Rename button exists but is not surfaced.

open_questions.summary: Computed CSS for line-height, letter-spacing, paragraph-spacing, contrast-ratio not measured against WCAG 2.2 1.4.12 thresholds. Visual inspection only.
open_questions.resolution_path: Run Lighthouse/axe against /chat/* and /settings/general; or use javascript_tool to extract getComputedStyle() on body and label elements.

open_questions.summary: Did not test the 'paste long source then ask for summary, attempt source recovery' scenario. Compound-interest chat is generative, not summarization.
open_questions.resolution_path: Paste a public document into a fresh test chat, request summary, audit source-recovery and omission-visibility. Avoided here to not consume billed context on synthetic tasks.

open_questions.summary: Sidebar state persisted across F5 reload; cross-session (logout/login) and cross-device (mobile/desktop) persistence not tested.
open_questions.resolution_path: Logout/login; or sign in on a second device and observe state inheritance.

open_questions.summary: Auditor was not in a self-reported constrained state. Section 3 / Section 6 findings still observable from any state, but the felt cost of the 4-click settings path is not validated.
open_questions.resolution_path: Human user in a low-bandwidth state attempts the dyslexia-friendly font change from a chat. Time it. Compare against the audit's click count.

positive_observations.summary: Sidebar collapse state persists across F5 reload.

+ menu compresses 8 power features into one one-click hub; collapsed sidebar reflows chat content rather than removing features. Section 5 'simple mode removes power' pattern does not manifest on claude.ai.
Chat font dropdown previews each option in its own face, including OpenDyslexic rendering for the 'Dyslexic friendly' option.

exit_criteria_check.report_obeys_audit_rules: report obeys Section 0 and Section 1 conventions (plain language, structured, scannable); not stress-tested against a human reader in a constrained state
